response.text:
# REFLECTION

r1c3 is in critical energy deficit (11.77/39 kWh = 30% SoC) while islanded with a 1 kW load, giving approximately 11.77 hours of autonomy before depletion. The peer network is in deadlock: r2c3 consistently rejects all outbound transfers citing its own reserve constraints (28–34% SoC, demanding 70% thresholds), while r1c4 repeatedly requests 0.5 kWh at normal urgency. Notably, r0c3 and r1c2 have recently signaled ACCEPT on urgent requests, suggesting potential reciprocal willingness among some peers. However, the broader pattern shows a microgrid-wide mutual-deficit scenario where all households are protecting their own autonomy windows. With no trust circles established and no grid restoration signal, r1c3 must adopt a strict self-preservation posture: reject all outbound transfers until SoC recovers above 60%, accept inbound offers from willing peers (r0c3, r1c2) to stabilize reserves, and escalate to aggregator/grid restoration as the only viable path out of deadlock.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.65
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Microgrid deadlock with mutual energy deficit. r1c3 at 30% SoC (critical); all peers rejecting outbound. r0c3 and r1c2 show reciprocal acceptance signals. Prioritize inbound reception from willing peers and self-preservation. Zero outbound sharing until SoC ≥ 65% or grid restoration confirmed. Escalate to aggregator intervention."
ttl_ticks: 12
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
